theorem:  mergesort_correct
sample:   2
attempt:  fix_4

[proof]
intro l
by_cases h : l.length < 2
· have hm : mergesort l = l := by
    unfold mergesort
    simp [h]
  constructor
  · rw [hm]
    cases l with
    | nil =>
      exact Sorted.nil
    | cons x xs =>
      cases xs with
      | nil =>
        exact Sorted.singleton
      | cons y ys =>
        simp at h
        linarith
  · rw [hm]
    exact lemma_hint_0 l (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq T |> fun _ => by infer_instance)) (lemma_hint_15 l (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq T |> fun _ => by infer_instance)))
· unfold mergesort
  simp [h]
  constructor
  · cases hmerge : merge (mergesort (left l)) (mergesort (right l)) with
    | nil =>
      exact Sorted.nil
    | cons x xs =>
      cases xs with
      | nil =>
        exact Sorted.singleton
      | cons y ys =>
        exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons Sorted.nil (by intro hlt; exact hlt.elim)) (by intro hlt; exact hlt.elim)
  · exact lemma_hint_0 (merge (mergesort (left l)) (mergesort (right l))) l (by rfl)

[lean error]

        · rw [hm]
          exact lemma_hint_0 l (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq T |> fun _ => by infer_instance)) (lemma_hint_15 l (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq T |> fun _ => by infer_instance)))
                                                                                  ^
      · unfold mergesort

      · unfold mergesort

              exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons Sorted.nil (by intro hlt; exact hlt.elim)) (by intro hlt; exact hlt.elim)

  Sorted.nil
has type
  Sorted []
but is expected to have type
  Sorted (?m.237 :: ?m.238)
in the application

  hlt
has type `?m.235.1 ?m.237 ?m.236` which does not have the necessary form.

has type `inst✝².toLT.1 y x` which does not have the necessary form.
